bio: Pir Mehr Ali Shah, Sufi scholar and poet from Punjab, British India. He belongs to the Chishtiya order, and is known as a Hanafi scholar who led the anti-Ahmadiyya movement. He wrote several books, most notably Saif Chishtiai, a polemical work criticizing the Ahmadiyya movement by Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.
bio_ar_original: بير مهر علي شاه ، باحث وشاعر صوفي من البنجاب، الهند البريطانية. ينتمي إلى الطريقة الجشتية، وهو معروف بأنه عالم حنفي قاد الحركة المناهضة للأحمدية. كتب عدة كتب أبرزها سيف جشتيائي وهو عمل جدلي ينتقد الحركة الأحمدية لميرزا غلام أحمد.

full: Muhammad ibn Murad Mehmed Fatih al-Uthman
titles: Al-Fatih · Sultan al-Azam

names_i18n.ja: メフメト2世

bio: The bearer of the good news is the Mujahid King and the invading Sultan Abu Al-Fath and His Excellency Muhammad Khan bin Murad bin Muhammad Al-Othmani, known for short as Muhammad II, and by his more famous title Muhammad Al-Fatih. He is the seventh Sultan of the House of Othman and the fifth to be titled Sultan among them after his father Murad, his grandfather Muhammad I, and his grandfather Bayezid and Murad. He is the second to be titled “Second” of the Sultans of the House of Othman, and the first to hold the title of “Caesar of the Romans” among the Muslim rulers in general and the Ottoman Sultans in particular. He is nicknamed “the bearer of glad tidings” due to the belief of the majority of Muslims that the Prophet Muhammad’s prophecy of the conquest of Constantinople was fulfilled by him. He was also nicknamed in Europe “the Great Turk” and “the Emperor of the Turks” due to the importance and greatness of his achievements and military victories that he achieved at the expense of the Christian forces, knowing that what is meant by “the Turk” Here it is “Muslim” in general, and not ethnically Turkish, because the two names meant the same thing in the European concept at the time.
bio_ar_original: صاحب البِشارة الملكُ المُجاهد والسُلطان الغازي أبو الفتح والمعالي محمد خان بن مراد بن محمد العُثماني ، ويُعرف اختصارًا باسم محمد الثاني، وبِلقبه الأشهر محمد الفاتح ؛ هو سابع سلاطين آل عُثمان وخامس من تلقَّب بِلقب سُلطانٍ بينهم بعد والده مراد وجدُّه محمد الأوَّل وجدَّاه بايزيد ومُراد، وثاني من لُقِّب بِالـ«ثاني» من سلاطين آل عُثمان، وأوَّل من حمل لقب «قيصر الروم» من الحُكَّام المُسلمين عُمومًا والسلاطين العثمانيين خُصوصًا. يُلقَّب بِـ«صاحب البِشارة» اعتقادًا من جُمهُور المُسلمين أنَّ نُبُوءة الرسول محمد القائلة بفتح القسطنطينية قد تحققت على يديه، كما لُقِّب في أوروپَّا بِـ«التُركي الكبير» و«إمبراطور التُرك» نظرًا لِأهمية وعظمة إنجازاته وانتصاراته العسكريَّة التي حققها على حساب القوى المسيحيَّة، علمًا بِأنَّ المقصود بِـ«التُركي» هُنا هو «المُسلم» عُمومًا، وليس التُركي عرقيًّا، لأنَّ التسميتين كانتا تعنيان شيئًا واحدًا في المفهوم الأوروپي آنذاك.

full: Sayyid Muhammad Mir ibn Qasim al-Lahori 'Mian Mir'
primaryTitle: Shaykh al-Punjab — The Saint Who Refused the Emperor
titles: Mian Mir (Master Mir) · Patron Saint of Lahore · Teacher of Dara Shikoh
bio: Saint Muhammad Moin Islam, known by the title Mian Mir (Hindi:), was a famous Muslim Sufi saint who resided in Lahore, specifically in the town of Dharampura. He was a direct descendant of Caliph Omar bin Al Khattab. He is famous for being the spiritual teacher of Dara Shikoh who is considered a son of the Mughal Emperor Nur al-Din Jahangir. He is identified as the founder of the Mian Khel branch of Sufism.
bio_ar_original: القديس محمد معين إسلام المعروف بلقب ميان مير (بالهندية: ، كان قديس صوفي مسلم مشهورًا أقام في لاهور، وتحديدًا في بلدة دارامبورا. وكان سليلاً مباشراً للخليفة عمر بن الخطاب. يشتهر بكونه المعلم الروحي لدارا شكوه الذي يعتبر ابن للإمبراطور المغولي نور الدين جهانكير. تم التعرف عليه على أنه مؤسس فرع ميان خيل من الصوفية

bio: Mihja bin Salah (c. 594–624 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Yeman. Known as Companion of Badr.

full: Yūsuf Sinān ibn ʿAbd al-Mannān, known as Mīmār Sinān
primaryTitle: Chief Ottoman Architect — Builder of the Süleymaniye and Selimiye
titles: Mīmār-ı Ḫāṣṣ (Chief Imperial Architect)
bio: Mimar Sinan also known as Koca Mi'mâr Sinân Âğâ, was the chief Ottoman architect, engineer and mathematician for sultans Suleiman the Magnificent, Selim II and Murad III. He was responsible for the construction of more than 300 major structures, including the Selimiye Mosque in Edirne, the Kanuni Sultan Suleiman Bridge in Büyükçekmece, and the Mehmed Paša Sokolović Bridge in Višegrad, as well as other more modest projects such as madrasa's, külliyes, and bridges. His apprentices would later design the Sultan Ahmed Mosque in Istanbul and the Stari Most bridge in Mostar.

full: Miqdad ibn Amr ibn Tha'laba al-Bahrani al-Kindi
titles: First Cavalryman of Islam · Among the First Six Muslims
bio: Miqdad ibn Amr ibn Tha'laba al-Bahrani al-Kindi (c. 585–653) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as First Cavalryman of Islam.

full: Muhammad Baqir ibn Shams al-Din Muhammad Damad
primaryTitle: Third Teacher after Aristotle and Al-Farabi
titles: al-Muallim al-Thalith · Sayyid al-Afadil
bio: Muhammad Baqir bin Muhammad al-Husseini al-Astarabadi. Al-Mirdamad is known as a scholar and philosopher from Persia. He is one of the great scholars and distinguished wise men of the Safavid era. Born in the tenth century AH, he was one of the founders of the Isfahan School of Shiite theology. His lineage goes back to Hussein bin Ali. The one who gave him the nickname “Al-Mirdamad” was his father, Mr. Muhammad Al-Damad Al-Astarabadi. He died in Najaf.
bio_ar_original: محمّد باقر بن محمّد الحسيني الاسترآبادي. المعروف الميرداماد هو عالم وفيلسوف من بلاد فارس و هو من العلماء الكبار وأصحاب الحكمة المتميزين في العهد الصفوي. ولد في القرن العاشر الهجري، كان من مؤسسي مدرسة أصفهان لعلم الكلام الشيعي يرجع نسبه إلى إلى الحسين بن علي. ان من أطلق عليه لقب «الميرداماد» هو والده السيد محمد الداماد الاسترابادي. توفي بالنجف.

full: Mir Sayyid Ali ibn Shihab al-Din Hamadani
titles: Shah-i Hamadan · Amir-i Kabir

names_i18n.zh: 米尔·赛义德·阿里·哈马多尼
names_i18n.ja: サイード・アリー・ハマドニ

_merge_notes: merged from F1954 on 20260422_193715: dob kept 1314; filled=[]
bio: Ali bin Shihab al-Din Hassan bin Muhammad al-Husseini al-Hamdhani, known as Ibn Shihab al-Hamdani al-Masoudi, is an Iranian scholar, poet, and Muslim Sufi saint of the Kubrawiyya order from the people of Khorasan. He was born in Hamadan, Iran and preached Islam in Central and South Asia while traveling to practice Sufism. He died in Khatlon Province, Tajikistan in 1384 at the age of 69-70. Al-Hamdani was addressed as honorifics throughout his life as Shah Hamdan, Amir Kabir, and Ali Sani.
bio_ar_original: علي بن شهاب الدين حسن بن محمد الحسيني الهمذاني المعروف باسم ابن شهاب الهمداني المسعودي هو عالم إيراني وشاعر وقديس صوفي مسلم من الطريقة الكبروية من أهل خراسان. ولد في مدينة همدان بإيران وبشر بالإسلام في وسط وجنوب آسيا أثناء سفره لممارسة الصوفية. توفي في ولاية خاتلون، طاجيكستان عام 1384 عن عمر يناهز 69-70 عامًا. كان يخاطب الحمداني بلفظ تشريف طوال حياته باسم شاه حمدان ، وأمير كبير ، وعلي ساني.

primaryTitle: God of Urdu Poetry — Khuda-e Sukhan
titles: Khudā-e Sukhan (God of Poetry)

names_i18n.ja: ミール・タキー・ミール

bio: Mir Taqi Mir, real name Muhammad Taqi (sometimes pronounced takhallus), was a pioneer Urdu poet of the 18th century, one of the pioneers who gave shape to the Urdu language. He was one of the seminal poets of the Delhi School of Urdu Ghazals, and it can be said that he remains the first name in the world of Urdu poetry and is often mentioned as...
bio_ar_original: مير تقي مير  ، اسمه الحقيقي محمد تقي و(takhallus) هو مير (وأحيانًا يُنطق ، هو شاعر أردي من رواد القرن الثامن عشر، وأحد الرواد الذين أعطوا للغة الأردية شكلها. وقد كان أحد الشعراء الأساسيين لمدرسة دلهي لـالغزل  الأردوي، ويمكن القول أنه بقي الاسم الأول في عالم الشعر الأردوي وغالبًا ما يتم ذكره بوصفه  .

full: Muḥammad ibn Khwānd Shāh, known as Mīrkhwānd
primaryTitle: Author of Rawzat al-Safa — Timurid Universal History

names_i18n.ja: ミールホーンド

bio: Muḥammad ibn Khwānd Shāh, known as Mīrkhwānd (1433–1498) was a scholar, based in Bukhara. Known as Author of Rawzat al-Safa — Timurid Universal History.

full: Mirza Asad Allah Khan Ghalib al-Dihlawi
titles: Ghalib (Victorious) · Asad (Lion) · Last Mughal court poet · Eyewitness of the fall of Delhi 1857

names_i18n.ja: ガーリブ

bio: Mirza Asadullah Beg Khan, commonly known as Mirza Ghalib, was an Indian poet and letter writer of the Mughal Empire. Writing in Persian and Urdu during the final years of the Mughal Empire and the rise of British colonial rule, his poetry often addressed themes of love, loss, philosophy, the human condition, and socio-political disturbances with a depth and complexity that influenced the literary traditions of his time. His ghazals, noted for their intricate imagery and layered meanings, form a significant part of Urdu literature. He spent most of his life in poverty.

full: Mirza Mazhar Jan-e-Janan Shamsuddin Habibullah al-Naqshbandi
primaryTitle: Shahid of Delhi — Teacher of Waliullah's Circle
titles: Mazhar Jan-e-Janan · Shahid of Delhi · Key teacher of the 18th century Delhi Sufi renaissance
bio: Mirza Mazhar Jan-e-Janan Shamsuddin Habibullah al-Naqshbandi (1699–1781) was a mystic of the Naqshbandiyya tradition, based in Delhi. Known as Shahid of Delhi — Teacher of Waliullah's Circle.

full: Abū ʿAlī Aḥmad ibn Muḥammad ibn Yaʿqūb Miskawayh

names_i18n.ja: アブー・アリー・ミスカワイヒ
names_i18n.uz: Ibn Miskavayh Abu Ali Ibn Muhammad

bio: Miskawayh Ahmad bin Muhammad bin Yaqoub Miskawayh, Abu Ali: Research historian, originally from Rayy, lived in Isfahan and died there. He worked in philosophy, chemistry, and logic for a while, then became interested in history, literature, and construction.
bio_ar_original: مسكويه أحمد بن محمد بن يعقوب مسكويه، أبو علي: مؤرخ بحاث، أصله من الري وسكن أصفهان وتوفي بها. اشتغل بالفلسفة والكيمياء والمنطق مدة، ثم أولع بالتاريخ والأدب والإنشاء.

famous: Mistah bin Uthatha b 'Abbad b al-Mutt
full: Mistah bin Uthatha b 'Abbad b al-Mutt

names_i18n.ar: مستح بن أثاثة بن عباد بن المطلب
names_i18n.ur: مستح بن اثاثہ بن عباد بن المطلب

bio: Mistah bin Uthatha b 'Abbad b al-Mutt (b. c. 594 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Companion of Badr.

primaryTitle: Reform President of Iran — Dialogue of Civilizations
bio: Sayyid Muḥammad Khātamī (1943–2025) was a ruler of the Islamic Philosophy tradition, based in Ardakan. Known as Reform President of Iran — Dialogue of Civilizations.

full: Muḥammad Taqī Bahār, known as Malik al-Shuʿarāʾ
primaryTitle: Poet Laureate of Iran — Last Great Classical Persian Poet
titles: Malik al-Shuʿarāʾ (King of Poets)

names_i18n.ja: モハンマド＝タギー・バハール

bio: Muhammad Taqi Bahar, an Iranian poet called the King of Poets. He has published collections, and has also worked on achieving some literary works.
bio_ar_original: محمد تقي بهار  ، شاعر إيراني يلقب بملك‌ الشعراء. له دواوين مطبوعة، كما عمل على تحقيق بعض الآثار الأدبية.

names_i18n.ja: ムハンマド・アルクーン

bio: Muḥammad Arkūn (1928–2010) was a scholar of the Islamic Philosophy tradition, based in Taourirt-Mimoun (Algeria). Known as Pioneer of Critical Islamic Studies.

full: Khwaja Mu'in al-Din Hasan al-Sijzi al-Chishti
primaryTitle: Gharib Nawaz — Benefactor of the Poor
titles: Gharib Nawaz (Benefactor of the Poor) · Khwaja-e-Khwajagan · Sultan al-Hind

names_i18n.ja: ムイーヌッディーン・チシュティー

bio: Al-Chishti Mu'in al-Din Hasan Sajzi (1143–1236), better known as Mu'in al-Din al-Chishti or by the nickname Gharib Nawaz, or as Sheikh Mu'in al-Din or Khawaja Mu'in al-Din, was a Sunni Muslim preacher, ascetic, religious scholar, philosopher, and mystic from Sistan, who eventually settled in the Indian subcontinent in the early 13th century where he spread the famous Chishti order of Sunni Sufism. This path became the dominant Islamic spiritual system in medieval India, and many Indian Sunni saints joined it, including Nizamuddin Auliya and Amir Khusraw.
bio_ar_original: الجشتي معين الدين حسن سجزي (1143–1236)، المعروف أكثر باسم معين الدين الجشتي أو بلقب غريب نواز ، أو باسم الشيخ معين الدين أو الخواجة معين الدين، كان واعظًا مسلمًا سنيًا وزاهدًا وعالمًا دينيًا وفيلسوفًا ومتصوفًا من سيستان، استقر في نهاية المطاف في شبه القارة الهندية في أوائل القرن الثالث عشر حيث نشر الطريقة الجشتية الشهيرة للتصوف السني. أصبحت هذه الطريقة هي النظام الروحي الإسلامي السائد في الهند في العصور الوسطى، وانضم إليها العديد من القديسين السنة الهنود، بما في ذلك نظام الدين أولياء وأمير خسرو.

primaryTitle: First Shaykh al-Islam of the Ottoman Empire
titles: Shaykh al-Islam al-Awwal · Molla Fenari

names_i18n.ja: モッラ・フェナーリー

bio: Muhammad bin Hamza bin Muhammad al-Fanari al-Rumi, also known as “Shams al-Din al-Fanari” or “Shams al-Din al-Fanari,” is a Turkish Muslim scholar, judge, interpreter, and scholar of logic and principles, a Hanafi jurist, and a Sheikh of Islam in the first Ottoman era. He was born, raised and educated in Fanar. Then he traveled to Egypt in 1376 AD, and learned from some of its scholars, including Akmal al-Din al-Babarti. He was the governor of Bursa and became famous with Sultan Bayezid I. He performed Hajj twice, the first time he visited Egypt in 1419 AD and the second in 1429 AD. He died after returning from Hajj. He is the author of “Ayn al-Ayyan fi Tafsir al-Qur’an,” “Al-Fawa’id al-Fanariyya,” “Misbah al-Uns,” “Awisat al-Afkar,” and “Fusoul al-Bada’i fi Usul al-Shara’i.”
bio_ar_original: محمد بن حمزة بن محمد الفَنّاري الرومي ، ويُعرف أيضا باسم «شمس الدين فناري» أو «شمس الدين الفناري»، عالم مسلم تركي وقاض ومفسر وعالم بالمنطق والأصول ومن فقهاء الحنفية وشيخ الإسلام في العهد العثماني الأول. ولد في فنار ونشأ وتعلم بها. ثم رحل إلى مصر 1376م فأخذ عن بعض علمائها منهم أكمل الدين البابرتي. ولي قضاء بورصة واشتهر عند السلطان بايزيد الأول وحج مرتين، زار في الأولى مصر سنة 1419م والثانية سنة 1429م وتوفي بعد عودته من الحج. له من مؤلفات «عين الأعيان في تفسير القرآن» و«الفوائد الفنارية» و«مصباح الأُنس» وعويصات الأفكار و«فصول البدائع في أصول الشرائع».

primaryTitle: From the First Pledge of Aqabah
bio: Mu'adh bin 'Afra' (b. c. 594 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as From the First Pledge of Aqabah.
Mu'adh bin 'Amr bin al-Jamuh (c. 594–650 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of Badr.
Mu'adh bin Ma'is b Qays (c. 594–626 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of Badr.
Mu'adha bint 'Abdullah bin 'Amr (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.
Mu'attib bin 'Auf (b. c. 594 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Companion of Badr.
Mu'attib bin 'Ubaid (c. 594–625 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of Badr.

titles: Amīr al-Muʾminīn · Ḥilm (the Forbearing)

names_i18n.ja: ムアーウィヤ

bio: Muʿāwiya ibn Abī Sufyān (c. 602–680) was a ruler of the Early Ascetics tradition, based in Damascus. Known as Founder of the Umayyad Caliphate.
Mu'awwidh bin 'Afra' (c. 594–624 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of Badr.
Mu'awwidh bin 'Amr bin al-Jamuh (b. c. 594 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of Badr.
Mu'ayqayb bin Abi Fatima al-Dausi (c. 593–650 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Emigrant to Abyssinia.
Mu'ttib bin Qushayr bin Mulayl (b. c. 594 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of Badr.

full: Muadh ibn Jabal ibn Amr al-Ansari al-Khazraji
primaryTitle: Imam al-Halal wa'l-Haram — Master of What is Permitted
titles: Imam al-Halal wa'l-Haram
bio: Abu Abd al-Rahman, Muadh ibn Jabal, al-Ansari, al-Khazraji, al-Madani, a companion, a jurist, a reciter of the Qur’an, a narrator of the Prophet’s hadith, from the Ansar, from the Banu Uday, from Banu Jashm ibn Al-Khazraj. He converted to Islam when he was 18 years old, and he witnessed the Second Pledge of Aqaba, then he witnessed all the scenes with the Prophet Muhammad, and he succeeded Muaz and Abu Musa Al-Ash’ari in Mecca after its conquest. They taught people the Qur’an and jurisprudence in religion, then used it on Yemen after the Battle of Tabuk. Then, after the death of the Prophet Muhammad, he witnessed the Islamic conquest in the Levant. He died in the Levant during the plague of Emmaus.
bio_ar_original: أَبُو عَبْدِ ٱلرَّحۡمَـٰنِ، مُعَاذُ بْنُ جَبَلٍ، ٱلْأَنْصَارِيُّ، ٱلْخَزْرَجِيُّ، ٱلْمَدَنِيُّ صَحابيٌّ، فقيه، قارئ للقرآن، راوٍ للحديث النبوي، من الأنصار، من بَنِي أُدَيٍّ، من بَنِي جُشَمَ بْنِ اَلْخَزْرَجِ. أسلم وهو ابْنُ 18 سنة، وشَهِد بيعة العقبة الثانية، ثم شهد مع النبي محمد المشاهد كلَّها، وخلَّفَ معاذًا وأبَا موسى الأشعري بمكةَ بعد فتحها؛ يُعلِّمُانِ الناسَ القرآنَ، والفقهَ في الدين، ثم استعمله على اليمن بعد غزوة تَبُوك. ثم شَهِدَ بعد وفاة النبي محمد الفتحَ الإسلامي بالشام. وتُوفي بالشام في طاعون عَمواس.

bio: Mubashshir bin 'Abdul Mundhir (c. 594–624 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of Badr.

primaryTitle: Companion of the Pledge of Ridwan — Quran 48:18
bio: Mubushir bin al-Bara' bin Ma'rur (b. c. 600 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of the Pledge of Ridwan — Quran 48:18.
Mudar ibn Nizār (c. 25 BCE–c. 40 CE) was a founding figure, based in Mecca. Known as Ancestor of Prophet Muhammad.
Mudlij bin 'Amr al-Salmi (b. c. 594 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Companion of Badr.

_merge_notes: merged from F1036 on 20260422_193715: dob kept 25; filled=[]
bio: Mudriqa bin Elias bin Mudar, whose name is Amer, the fifteenth grandfather of the Prophet Muhammad bin Abdullah, the master of the Arabs in the Hijaz, and one of the great nobles of the Mudriqa tribes. His nickname is Abu Hudhail.
bio_ar_original: مدركة بن إلياس بن مضر وأسمه عامر الجد الخامس عشر للنبي محمد بن عبد الله وسيد العرب في الحجاز وأحد أشرافها الكبار وجد قبائل مدركة. يكنى أبو هذيل.

full: Muḥammad ʿAbduh Ibrāhīm Khudra Abū al-ʿIlm
primaryTitle: Grand Mufti of Egypt — Father of Islamic Modernism
titles: Grand Mufti of Egypt

names_i18n.ja: ムハンマド・アブドゥフ

bio: Muhammad Abduh, an Egyptian Islamic thinker, religious scholar, jurist, judge, and writer, is considered one of the advocates of renaissance and reform in the Arab and Islamic world, and a symbol of renewal in Islamic jurisprudence. After meeting with his teacher, Jamal al-Din al-Afghani, he contributed to the establishment of an Islamic renewal intellectual movement in the late nineteenth century and the beginning of the twentieth century, aiming to eliminate intellectual and civilizational stagnation and revitalize the Islamic nation to keep pace with the requirements of the times.
bio_ar_original: محمد عبده ، مفكر إسلامي وعالم دين مصري، وفقيه وقاض وكاتب مجدد، يعد أحد دعاة النهضة والإصلاح في العالم العربي والإسلامي، ورمز التجديد في الفقه الإسلامي، أسهم بعد التقائه بأستاذه جمال الدين الأفغاني في إنشاء حركة فكرية تجديدية إسلامية في أواخر القرن التاسع عشر وبدايات القرن العشرين تهدف إلى القضاء على الجمود الفكري والحضاري وإعادة إحياء الأمة الإسلامية لتواكب متطلبات العصر.

full: Muhammad ibn Ali ibn al-Husayn ibn Ali ibn Abi Talib al-Hashimi
primaryTitle: Al-Baqir — The Splitter of Knowledge; 5th Imam and Father of Islamic Sciences
classif: Imam / Scholar / Jurist / Son of Zayn al-Abidin
titles: Al-Baqir (Splitter/Disseminator of Knowledge) · Abu Jafar · 5th Imam in Shia tradition · Father of Islamic jurisprudence in the Alid tradition
teachers: Jabir ibn Abd Allah al-Ansari (last surviving companion)
quranRef: Q33:33 (verse of purification, revealed for his family)

names_i18n.ja: ムハンマド・バーキル

bio: Abu Jafar Muhammad bin Ali bin Al-Hussein bin Ali bin Abi Talib, Al-Hashimi, Al-Alawi, Al-Madani, Al-Qurashi, Al-Baqir The fifth imam according to the Imami Shiites and (the Ismailis) and one of the infallibles and the Ahl al-Bayt according to the Shiite belief. His father is Ali bin Al-Hussein bin Ali bin Abi Talib and his mother is Fatima bint Al-Hassan bin Ali bin Abi Talib.
bio_ar_original: أَبُو جَعْفَرِِ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنُ ٱلْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ، ٱلْهَاشِمِيُّ، ٱلْعَلَوِيُّ ، ٱلْمَدَنِيُّ، ٱلْقُرَشِيُّ، الْبَاقِرِ الإمام الخامس عند الشيعة الإمامية و(الإسماعيليين) ومن المعصومين وأهل البيت في اعتقاد الشيعة. والده علي بن الحسين بن علي بن أبي طالب وأمه فاطمة بنت الحسن بن علي بن أبي طالب.

full: Al-Hajj Umar ibn Said Tall al-Futi
primaryTitle: Al-Hajj Umar Tal — Jihad of the Pen and Sword
titles: Al-Hajj Umar · Amir al-Muminin
bio: Al-Hajj Umar ibn Said Tall al-Futi (1794–1864) was a historical figure of the Qadiriyya tradition, based in Futa Toro. Known as Al-Hajj Umar Tal — Jihad of the Pen and Sword.

full: Muhammad ibn Ali al-Ridha al-Jawad al-Taqi al-Hashimi
primaryTitle: Imam al-Jawad — Ninth Imam; Youngest Imam at Age 9
titles: al-Jawad (The Generous) · al-Taqi (The God-Fearing) · Ninth Imam

names_i18n.ja: ムハンマド・タキー

bio: Abu Jaafar Muhammad bin Ali Al-Jawad is the ninth of the twelve imams of the Twelver Shiites, and he is the eleventh of the fourteen infallibles. He was a contemporary of two of the Abbasid caliphs, Al-Ma’mun and Al-Mu’tasim, and he died during the latter’s caliphate. He was also a contemporary of the Waqifi sect, which believes that the imamate stopped with his grandfather, Musa al-Kadhim, and does not believe in the imamate after him. (1)
bio_ar_original: أبو جعفر محمد بن علي الجواد ، هو التاسع من إثني عشر إماماً من أئمة الشيعة الإثني عشرية، وهو الحادي عشر من المعصومين الأربعة عشر، وقد عاصر اثنين من الخلفاء العباسيين هما المأمون، والمعتصم، وتوفي في خلافة الأخير. كما عاصر الفرقة الواقفية التي تعتقد بتوقف الإمامة عند جدّه موسى الكاظم، ولا تعتقد بإمامة من بعده.(1)

primaryTitle: Imam al-Salat — Reviver of the Shadhili Path
titles: Imam al-Salat · Shaykh al-Tariqa
bio: Abu Abdullah Muhammad bin Suleiman bin Abu Bakr Al-Jazouli Al-Samlali Al-Hasani, often known as Imam Al-Jazouli or Sheikh Al-Jazouli, is known for his compilation of the book Dala’il Al-Khairat wa Shariq Al-Anwar fi Dhikr Al-Salaat upon the Chosen Prophet, which is a book of dhikr for Muslims that is very popular. This book is usually divided into 7 sections for each day of the week. Al-Jazouli is one of the Seven Scholars of Marrakesh.
bio_ar_original: أبو عبد الله محمد بن سليمان بن أبو بكر الجزولي السملالي الحسني ، وغالبا ما يعرف باسم الإمام الجزولي أو الشيخ الجزولي، وهو معروف بتجميعه لكتاب دلائل الخيرات وشوارق الأنوار في ذكر الصلاة على النبي المختار، وهو كتاب للذكر للمسلمين له شعبية كبيرة. ينقسم هذا الكتاب عادة إلى 7 أقسام لكل يوم من أيام الأسبوع. الجزولي هو واحد من العلماء السبعة في مراكش.

full: Muḥammad ibn ʿAlī al-Sanūsī al-Khattābī al-Ḥasanī
primaryTitle: Founder of the Sanusiyya — Builder of the Libyan Islamic State
titles: Al-Sayyid al-Mahdi · Imam al-Sanusiyya
bio: Muḥammad ibn ʿAlī al-Sanūsī al-Khattābī al-Ḥasanī (1787–1859) was a mystic of the Sanusiyya tradition, based in Algeria. Known as Founder of the Sanusiyya — Builder of the Libyan Islamic State.

primaryTitle: Shaybani — Systematiser of Hanafi Law and Father of Islamic International Law
titles: Shaykh al-Islam · Imam al-Iraq

names_i18n.ja: シャイバーニー

bio: Muhammad Shaybani Khan was an Uzbek leader who consolidated various Uzbek tribes and laid the foundations for their ascendance in Transoxiana and the establishment of the Khanate of Bukhara. He was a Shaybanid or descendant of Shiban. He was the son of Shah-Budag, thus a grandson of the Uzbek conqueror Abu'l-Khayr Khan.

primaryTitle: Imam al-'Ilal — Master of Hadith Analysis
titles: Imam al-'Ilal · One of the Six Masters of Hadith
bio: Abu 'Isa Muhammad ibn 'Isa al-Tirmidhi al-Sulami (824–892) was a scholar of the Hadith Sciences tradition, based in Tirmidh (Termez). Known as Imam al-'Ilal — Master of Hadith Analysis.

primaryTitle: Born Muslim — First Generation Born into Islam

names_i18n.ar: محمد بن أبي حذيفة بن عتبة
names_i18n.ur: محمد بن ابی حذیفہ بن عتبہ

bio: Muhammad bin Abu Hudhayfah ibn 'Utbah (b. c. 595 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Abyssinia. Known as Born Muslim — First Generation Born into Islam.
Muhammad bin Hatib bin al-Harith (c. 593–705 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Emigrant to Abyssinia.

primaryTitle: Born Muslim — First Generation Born into Islam
bio: Muhammad bin Hattab bin al-Harith (b. c. 593 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Born Muslim — First Generation Born into Islam.

titles: Qutb al-Aqtab · Ghaus-i Azam Hind
bio: Muhammad Ghaus ibn Khattu al-Gwaliari (c. 1500–1563) was a mystic of the Suhrawardiyya tradition, based in Gwalior. Known as Qutb of the Shatariyya.

titles: Dr. Muhammad Hamidullah
bio: Muhammad Hamidullah Al-Haiderabadi, one of the great figures of Arab Islamic culture in the modern era, is a hadith scholar, jurist, and one of the greatest scholars and preachers that the Indian subcontinent in general has produced. He holds a doctorate in philosophy and obtained two doctorates in the biography of the Prophet from two different universities, one from the University of Paris and the other from the University of Tübingen in Germany. He spent nearly half his life researching and investigating Europe and the Middle East. A prolific writer, he has many academic studies and extensive works on Islamic sciences, history and culture that are unprecedented in the last century, published in several languages ​​with thousands of articles in scientific journals. He was the first Muslim to provide a translation of the meanings of the Holy Qur’an in French, which is considered one of the best translations of the meanings of the Qur’an ever. He also translated the interpretation of the Holy Qur’an into many other languages. He speaks many languages ​​fluently, such as: Urdu, Persian, Arabic, French, English, German, Italian, Greek, Turkish, Russian, and others, up to 17 languages. He even learned the Thai language when he was eighty-four years old. He has more than 250 valuable books in these languages. He has hundreds of articles on the Qur’an, the Prophet’s biography, jurisprudence, history, law, writings, and more.
bio_ar_original: محمد حميد الله الحيدرآبادي ، أحد أعلام الثقافة العربية الإسلامية الكبار في العصر الحديث، هو محدّث وفقيه وأحد كبار العلماء والدعاة الذين أنجبتهم شبه القارة الهندية بصورة عامة. حاصل على دكتوراة في الفلسلفة كما حصل على درجتي الدكتوراة في السيرة النبوية من جامعتين مختلفتين أحداهما من جامعة باريس والأخرى من جامعة توبنجن بألمانيا. قضى ما يقرب من نصف عمره بالبحث والتحقيق في أوروبا ودول الشرق الأوسط. كاتب غزير الإنتاج، وله العديد من الدراسات الأكاديمية والأعمال الواسعة في العلوم الإسلامية والتاريخ والثقافة غير المسبوقة في القرن الماضي، وقد تم نشرها بعدة لغات مع آلاف المقالات في المجلات العلمية. وكان أول مسلم يقدم ترجمة لمعاني القرآن الكريم باللغة الفرنسية والتي تعد من أفضل ترجمات معاني القرآن على الإطلاق، كما قام بترجمة تفسير القرآن الكريم للعديد من اللغات الأخرى. يتحدث الكثير من اللغات بطلاقة مثل: الأردية ، الفارسية، العربية، الفرنسية، الإنجليزية، الألمانية، الإيطالية، اليونانية، التركية، الروسية وغيرها تصل إلى 17 لغة حتى أنه تعلم اللغة التايلاندية وهو في الأربع والثمانين من عمره. وله مؤلفات قيمة بهذه اللغات يبلغ عددها أكثر من 250 كتاباً. وله مئات المقالات في القرآن والسيرة النبوية والفقه والتاريخ والحقوق والمكاتيب وغيرها.

full: Muhammad ibn Yusuf ibn Nasr ibn al-Ahmar al-Nasri al-Andalusi
primaryTitle: Ibn al-Ahmar — Founder of Nasrid Dynasty and the Alhambra
titles: Ibn al-Ahmar · Muhammad al-Ghalib · No Victor but God

names_i18n.ja: ムハンマド1世

bio: Abu Abdullah Muhammad I is the victor by God, Muhammad bin Yusuf bin Muhammad bin Ahmed bin Nasr bin Al-Ahmar, known as Ibn Al-Ahmar. From the Banu Nasr or Banu al-Ahmar, descended from the Khazraj Azadi Qahtani tribe, the founder of the Banu Nasr state in Andalusia and its ruling dynasty. He ruled the Kingdom of Granada between A.D.
bio_ar_original: أبو عبد الله محمد الأول هو الغالب بالله محمد بن يوسف بن محمد بن أحمد بن نصر بن الأحمر، المعروف بـابن الأحمر. من بني نصر أو بني الأحمر المنحدرة من قبيلة الخزرج الأزدية القحطانية، مؤسس دولة بني نصر بالأندلس والسلالة الحاكمة فيها. حكم مملكة غرناطة بين عامي.

full: Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Abi Talib al-Hashimi (known as Ibn al-Hanafiyya)
primaryTitle: Son of Ali ibn Abi Talib — Respected Scholar
titles: Ibn al-Hanafiyya · Imam of Knowledge

names_i18n.zh: 穆罕默德·伊本·哈納菲耶
names_i18n.ja: ムハンマド＝イブン・アル・ハナフィーヤ

bio: Muhammad bin Ali bin Abi Talib Al-Hashimi Al-Qurashi. His mother was Khawla bint Jaafar Al-Hanafiyya, so he is attributed to her distinctly from his brothers Al-Hasan and Al-Hussein. He was born during the caliphate of Omar bin Al-Khattab in the year twenty-one AH. He was one of the staunch heroes. He was pious, knowledgeable and trustworthy, and had several hadiths in the Sahihs.
bio_ar_original: محمد بن علي بن أبي طالب الهاشمي القرشي أمه خولة بنت جعفر الحنفية فينسب إليها تمييزاً عن أخويه الحسن والحسين، ولد في خلافة عمر بن الخطاب سنة إحدى وعشرين للهجرة، وهو أحد الأبطال الأشداء، كان ورعاً واسع العلم ثقة له عدة أحاديث في الصحيحين.

titles: Conqueror of Sind

names_i18n.ja: ムハンマド・ビン・カシム

bio: Muḥammad ibn al-Qāsim al-Thaqafī was an Arab military commander in service of the Umayyad Caliphate who led the Muslim conquest of Sindh, inaugurating the Umayyad campaigns in India. His military exploits led to the establishment of the Islamic province of Sindh, and the takeover of the region from the Sindhi Brahman dynasty and its ruler, Raja Dahir, who was subsequently decapitated with his head sent to al-Hajjaj ibn Yusuf in Basra. With the capture of the then-capital of Aror by Arab forces, Muhammad ibn al-Qasim became the first Muslim to have successfully captured Indian land, which marked the beginning of Muslim rule in South Asia.

full: Muhammad ibn Maslama ibn Khalid al-Ansari al-Awsi al-Harithiyyi
primaryTitle: Trusted Special Envoy and Swordsman of the Prophet
titles: Abu Abd Allah · Trusted Special Envoy of the Prophet · Swordsman of the Ansar

names_i18n.zh: 穆罕默德·伊本·麦斯莱拉

bio: Muhammad ibn Maslama, a companion of Banu Haritha ibn al-Harith from Aws, was an ally of Banu Abd al-Ashhal. He converted to Islam in the past, witnessed all the scenes except the Battle of Tabuk, participated in the Islamic conquest of Egypt, and was the special envoy of Omar bin Al-Khattab to his governors. Muhammad ibn Maslama retired from the strife and lived in Al-Rabadha until he was killed during the caliphate of Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan.
bio_ar_original: محمد بن مسلمة صحابي من بني حارثة بن الحارث من الأوس، كان حليفًا لبني عبد الأشهل. أسلم قديمًا، وشهد المشاهد كلها إلا غزوة تبوك، وشارك في الفتح الإسلامي لمصر، وكان المبعوث الخاص لعمر بن الخطاب إلى ولاته. اعتزل محمد بن مسلمة الفتنة، وسكن الربذة إلى أن قُتل في خلافة معاوية بن أبي سفيان.

full: Muhammad ibn Shihab ibn Abd Allah al-Zuhri al-Qurashi
primaryTitle: Imam al-Hadith — Father of Systematic Hadith Collection
titles: Imam al-Hadith · Father of Systematic Hadith Compilation · Ibn Shihab
bio: Pioneer of hadith compilation and maghazi literature. Served as court scholar under the Umayyads, tutoring princes at Resafa for two decades. Studied under leading Medinan Tabi'in and transmitted to the founders of major hadith and sira traditions. Regarded as one of the greatest hadith authorities of his generation.
tribe_or_lineage: Banu Zuhrah (clan of Quraysh)
key_events: Studied under four great Medinan scholars in his twenties
Gained Umayyad patronage under Abd al-Malik c.701 CE
Tasked by Umar II with compiling hadith into standardized books (717-720 CE)
Served as court tutor at Resafa under Caliph Hisham (724-743 CE)
full_name_ar: أبو بكر محمد بن مسلم بن عبيد الله بن عبد الله بن شهاب الزهري

full: Muhammad Yusuf Abu al-Mahasin al-Taj al-Khalwati al-Maqassari
primaryTitle: Tuanta Salamaka — Our Master the Blessed
titles: Tuanta Salamaka · Shaykh Yusuf
bio: Muhammad Yusuf Abu al-Mahasin al-Taj al-Khalwati al-Maqassari (1626–1699) was a mystic of the Naqshbandiyya tradition, based in Gowa. Known as Tuanta Salamaka — Our Master the Blessed.

full: Muhammad Bello ibn Uthman dan Fodio al-Fulani
primaryTitle: Sultan of Sokoto — Son of Usman dan Fodio
titles: Sultan of Sokoto · Amir al-Mu'minin

names_i18n.ja: ムハンマド・ベロ

bio: Muhammad Bello was the Sultan of the Sokoto Sultanate, born in 1781 in Nigeria, and died in the same place in 1837.
bio_ar_original: محمد بيلو هو سلطان سلطنة صكتو، ولد في 1781 في نيجيريا، وتوفي بنفس المكان في 1837.

primaryTitle: Born Muslim — First Generation Born into Islam
bio: Muhmmad bin Ash'uth bin Qays (b. c. 595 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Yemen. Known as Born Muslim — First Generation Born into Islam.
Muhriz bin 'Amir bin Malik (c. 594–625 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of Badr.
Muhriz bin Nadla al-Asadi (b. c. 594 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Companion of Badr.

primaryTitle: Imam al-Tafsir — Master of Quranic Exegesis
titles: Imam al-Tafsir · Student Who Read Quran with Ibn Abbas Thirty Times

names_i18n.ja: ムジャーヒド・ブン・ジャブル

bio: Abu Al-Hajjaj Mujahid bin Jabr bin Abdullah bin Nufayl bin Hilal bin Kinana bin Hammad bin Abbad bin Qasa bin Taghlib. The servant of Al-Sa'ib bin Abi Al-Sa'ib Al-Makhzoumi Al-Qurashi. He is known by his abbreviation in traditional sources and books as Mujahid. He is a Tabi’i, an imam, a jurist, a trustworthy scholar, and an extensive hadith scholar. He excelled in interpretation and reading of the Holy Qur’an and the Prophet’s hadith.
bio_ar_original: أبو الحجاج مُجاهِد بْن جَبْر بن عبد الله بن نفيل بن هلال بن كنانة بن حماد بن عباد بن قصعة بن تغلب. مولى السائب بن أبي السائب المخزومي القرشي. ويعرف اختصاراً في المصادر والكتب التراثية بمجاهد. وهو تابعيٌّ وإمامٌ وفقيهٌ وعالمُ ثقة وكثير الحديث، وبرز في التفسير وقراءة القرآن الكريم والحديث النبوي.

bio: Mulayka bint 'Abdullah b. Sakhr (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.
Mulayka bint 'Abdullah ibn Ubayy (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.
Mulayka bint 'Amr (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Female Narrator of Hadith.
Mulayka bint Sahl bin Zayd (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.
Mulayka bint Thabit (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.
Mulayl bin Wabara bin Khalid (b. c. 594 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of Badr.

full: Muhammad ibn Shah Murtada ibn Mahmud al-Kashani
titles: Al-Fayd al-Kashani · Al-Muhaddith
bio: Muhammad ibn Shah Murtada ibn Mahmud al-Kashani (1598–1680) was a scholar of the Islamic Philosophy tradition, based in Kashan. Known as Al-Muhaddith al-Fayd — The Inspired Traditionist.

full: Sadr al-Din Muhammad ibn Ibrahim Shirazi 'Mulla Sadra'
primaryTitle: Sadr al-Muta'allihin — Founder of Transcendent Wisdom
titles: Sadr al-Muta'allihin (Foremost of Divine Philosophers) · Founder of al-Hikma al-Muta'aliya (Transcendent Theosophy)

names_i18n.ja: モッラー・サドラー

bio: Mullah Sadra Muhammad bin Ibrahim Al-Qawami Al-Shirazi. He is Sadr al-Din al-Shirazi, known as Sadr al-Mutalahin, a sage and Twelver Shiite Muslim philosopher who was born in the late tenth century AH in Shiraz. He is the founder of the school of transcendent wisdom with his basic theory of the originality of existence and the essentiality of essence.
bio_ar_original: ملا صدرا محمد بن إبراهيم القوامي الشيرازي'. هو صدر الدين الشيرازي والمعروف بصدر المتألهين حكيم وفيلسوف مسلم شيعي إثنى عشري ولد أواخر القرن القرن العاشر الهجري في شيراز، وهو مؤسس مدرسة الحكمة المتعالية بنظريته الأساسية أصالة الوجود وإعتبارية الماهية.

bio: Muqawwam ibn Nahur (-1400–-1480) was a founding figure, based in Arabia. Known as Islamic History Founder of Arabia.

full: Murad ibn Muhammad ibn Bayezid al-Uthman al-Ghazi
primaryTitle: Ghazi Sultan — Father of Mehmed the Conqueror
titles: Murad Khan · Ghazi · Father of Mehmed the Conqueror

names_i18n.ja: ムラト2世

bio: Murad ibn Muhammad ibn Bayezid al-Uthman al-Ghazi (1404–1451) was a ruler, based in Amasya. Known as Ghazi Sultan — Father of Mehmed the Conqueror.
Murra ibn Ka'b (c. 260–c. 335 CE) was a founding figure, based in Mecca. Known as Ancestor of Prophet Muhammad.
Murrah ibn Ka'b (255–-340) was a founding figure, based in Mecca. Known as Islamic History Founder of Mecca.

primaryTitle: Companion of the Pledge of Ridwan — Quran 48:18
bio: Murri bin Sinan (b. c. 600 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of the Pledge of Ridwan — Quran 48:18.

full: Mus'ab ibn Umayr ibn Hashim ibn Abd al-Dar al-Qurashi
primaryTitle: Safeer al-Islam — First Ambassador of Islam
titles: Safeer (Envoy of the Prophet)
quranRef: Q33:23 — 'Among the believers are men true to their pledge'

names_i18n.ja: ムスアブ・イブン・ウマイル

bio: Abu Abdullah Musab bin Umair Al-Abdari was one of Badr’s first companions to Islam, the envoy of the Prophet Muhammad to call to Islam in Yathrib after the first Pledge of Aqaba, and the standard bearer of the Emigrants in the Battles of Badr and Uhud.
bio_ar_original: أبو عبد الله مصعب بن عمير العبدري صحابي بدري من السابقين إلى الإسلام، ومبعوث النبي محمد للدعوة إلى الإسلام في يثرب بعد بيعة العقبة الأولى، وحامل لواء المهاجرين في غزوتي بدر وأحد.

primaryTitle: Kalim Allah — He Who Spoke Directly with God
titles: Musa · Kalim Allah · Khatim al-Anbiya al-Isra'iliyyin · Moses · Kalimu'llah
dating_source: Merneptah Stele; Ramesside Egypt; Kitchen
dating_note: Musa/Moses; Late Bronze Exodus dating (~1250 BCE most common).

names_i18n.ja: イスラームにおけるモーセ

bio: Mūsā ibn 'Imrān (c. 1393–c. 1273 BCE) was a prophet, based in Egypt. Known as Kalim Allah — He Who Spoke Directly with God.

primaryTitle: Born Muslim — First Generation Born into Islam
bio: Musa bin al-Harith bin Khalid (b. c. 593 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Born Muslim — First Generation Born into Islam.

primaryTitle: Sufi commentator and expositor of Ibn Arabi's thought
bio: Abu Abdullah Musab bin Umair Al-Abdari was one of Badr’s first companions to Islam, the envoy of the Prophet Muhammad to call to Islam in Yathrib after the first Pledge of Aqaba, and the standard bearer of the Emigrants in the Battles of Badr and Uhud.
bio_ar_original: أبو عبد الله مصعب بن عمير العبدري صحابي بدري من السابقين إلى الإسلام، ومبعوث النبي محمد للدعوة إلى الإسلام في يثرب بعد بيعة العقبة الأولى، وحامل لواء المهاجرين في غزوتي بدر وأحد.

famous: Mushin bin Abi Qays bin al-Aslat
full: Mushin bin Abi Qays bin al-Aslat

names_i18n.ar: مسحين بن أبي قيس بن الأسلت
names_i18n.ur: مسحین بن ابی قیس بن الاسلت

bio: Mushin bin Abi Qays bin al-Aslat (b. c. 595 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Poet-Companion.

primaryTitle: Director of The Message and Omar Mukhtar — Islamic Cinema Pioneer

names_i18n.ja: ムスタファ・アッカド

bio: Mustafa Akkad is a Syrian-American film director and producer. He was the first Arab to arrive in Hollywood, where he produced the American horror film series Halloween in the 1970s. Then he moved on to produce more serious films about the Arab and Islamic world, such as: the film The Message in 1976, and the film Omar Al-Mukhtar in 1981.
bio_ar_original: مصطفى العقاد مخرج ومنتج أفلام أمريكي سوري. كان أولَ عربي يصل الى هوليوود وفيها أنتج سلسلة أفلام الرعب الأمريكية هالوين في السبعينيات، ثم انتقل لإنتاج أفلام أكثر جدية عن العالم العربي والإسلامي مثل: فلم الرسالة عام 1976، وفلم عمر المختار عام 1981.

primaryTitle: Last Shaykh al-Islam of the Ottoman Empire
titles: Shaykh al-Islam · Mustafa Sabri
bio: Mustafa Sabri was the last Sheikh of Islam in the Ottoman Empire. He is known for his views condemning the Turkish nationalist movement led by Kemal Ataturk. Because of his resistance to Ataturk, he lived half his life in exile in various countries, and died in Egypt.
bio_ar_original: مصطفى صبري كان آخر شيخ الإسلام في الدولة العثمانية. وهو معروف بآرائه التي تدين الحركة القومية التركية بقيادة كمال أتاتورك. بسبب مقاومته لأتاتورك، عاش نصف حياته في المنفى في مختلف البلدان، وتوفي في مصر.

bio: Muti'a bint al-Nu'man bin Malik (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.
Na'ila bint 'Ubaid bin al-Hurr (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.
Na'ila bint Rabi' bin Qays (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.
Na'ila bint Sa'd bin Malik (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.
Na'ila bint Salaama bin Waqash (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.
Na'ila bint al-Furafisa was the wife of the third caliph Uthman. She narrated many hadith including eyewitness accounts of the assassination of Uthman.

primaryTitle: Companion of the Pledge of Ridwan — Quran 48:18
bio: Na'im bin Maqrn al-Mazni (b. c. 600 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Hijaz. Known as Companion of the Pledge of Ridwan — Quran 48:18.
Nabia bin 'Uthman (b. c. 593 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Emigrant to Abyssinia.
Nābit ibn Ismā'īl (legendary–legendary) was a founding figure, based in Mecca. Known as Ancestor of Prophet Muhammad.
Nadba client of Maymuna (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Hijaz. Known as Female Narrator of Hadith.

full: Nadr Qulī Beg Afshār, known as Nādir Shāh
primaryTitle: Napoleon of Persia — Conqueror of Delhi
titles: Ẓill Allāh (Shadow of God)

names_i18n.ja: ナーディル・シャー

bio: Nader Shah Afshar was the founder of the Afsharid dynasty of Iran and one of the most powerful rulers in Iranian history, ruling as the emperor of Iran (Persia) from 1736 to 1747, when he was assassinated during a rebellion. He fought numerous campaigns throughout the Middle East, the Caucasus, Central Asia, and South Asia, emerging victorious from the battles of Herat, Mihmandust, Murche-Khort, Kirkuk, Yeghevārd, Khyber Pass, Karnal, and Kars. Nader belonged to the Turkoman Afshars, one of the seven Qizilbash tribes that helped the Safavid dynasty establish their power in Iran.

full: Nafi' al-Madani, Mawla Abd Allah ibn Umar al-Khattab
primaryTitle: The Golden Chain — Nafi' from Ibn Umar from the Prophet
titles: al-Isnad al-Dhahab (Golden Chain) · Most Trusted Medinan Transmitter
bio: Abu Abdullah Nafi’ Al-Madani, servant of Abdullah bin Omar bin Al-Khattab, one of the imams of the Successors in Medina, and one of the trustworthy narrators of the Prophet’s hadith. He narrated on the authority of Ibn Omar and other companions. He was knowledgeable in jurisprudence, and he narrated many hadiths. Malik bin Anas is considered the most famous of those who followed him and narrated from him. It was said about him: “He is not known to have made any mistakes in everything he narrated.” His talk in the six books. He died in the year 117 AH.
bio_ar_original: أبو عبد الله نافع المدني مولى عبد الله بن عمر بن الخطاب، من أئمة التابعين بالمدينة المنورة، وأحد رواة الحديث النبوي الثقات، روى عن ابن عمر وغيره من الصحابة، كان عالما بالفقه، كثير الرواية للحديث، ويعد مالك بن أنس أشهر من لازمه وحدث عنه. وقيل عنه: «لايعرف له خطأ في جميع ما رواه». حديثه في الكتب الستة. توفي سنة 117هـ.

full: Nafi' ibn Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Nu'aym al-Laythi al-Madani
primaryTitle: Imam al-Qurra — Master of Quranic Recitation
titles: Imam al-Qurra · One of the Seven Canonical Reciters

names_i18n.ja: ナーフィイ・マダニー

bio: Nafi bin Abd al-Rahman bin Abi Nu`aym al-Laythi al-Kinani, servant of Ja`unah bin Shu`ub al-Laythi al-Kinani, the imam, the ink of the Qur’an, one of the ten reciters, and the imam of reciters in the Prophet’s city. He is originally from Isfahan. He was born around 70 AH during the caliphate of Abd al-Malik bin Marwan, and it is said that it was the year seventy-five. Medani, named after the city of the Messenger of God, and he is from the third class after the Companions.
bio_ar_original: نافع بن عبد الرحمن بن أبي نُعيم الليثي الكناني مولى جعونة بن شعوب الليثي الكناني، الإمام حبر القرآن وأحد القراء العشرة وإمام القراء في المدينة النبوية، أصله من أصفهان، ولد في حدود 70 هـ في خلافة عبد الملك بن مروان ويقال سنة بضع وسبعين. مدنيّ نسبة إلى مدينة رسول الله، وهو من الطبقة الثالثة بعد الصحابة.

full: Nafisa bint al-Hasan ibn Zayd ibn al-Hasan ibn Ali
titles: Nafisa al-'Ilm · Sayyida
bio: Nafisa bint al-Hasan ibn Zayd ibn al-Hasan ibn Ali (762–824) was a mystic of the Sufi tradition, based in Medina. Known as Nafisa al-'Ilm — Jewel of Knowledge.
Nafisa bint Umayya al-Tamimia (b. c. 594 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Female Narrator of Hadith.
Nāhūr ibn Sārūgh (legendary–legendary) was a founding figure, based in Mesopotamia. Known as Ancestor of Prophet Muhammad. The sources record 2 teachers.
Nahur ibn Tayrah (-1440–-1530) was a founding figure, based in Arabia. Known as Islamic History Founder of Arabia.

full: Shaykh Najm al-Din Abu al-Jannab Ahmad al-Kubra
titles: Wali Tarash (Manufacturer of Saints) · Abu'l-Jannab · Qutb al-Khwarazm

names_i18n.ja: ナイムディン・クブラ

bio: Najm al-Din al-Kubra, a Sufi scholar from the people of Khorezm, Uzbekistan, and the founder of the Kubrawi Sufi order. Ibn Nuqtah said about him: “He is a Shafi’i, an imam in the Sunnah,” and al-Dhahabi described him as: “The sheikh, imam, scholar, role model, hadith scholar, and martyr sheikh of Khorasan.”
bio_ar_original: نجم الدين الكبرى ، عالم متصوف من أهل خوارزم أوزبكستان، ومؤسس الطريقة الكبرويَّة الصوفية، قال عنه ابن نقطة: «هو شافعي، إمام في السنّة»، ووصفه الذهبي بأنّه: «الشيخ الإمام العلامة القدوة المحدّث الشهيد شيخ خراسان».

primaryTitle: Father of Ottoman Turkish Literature and Nationalism

names_i18n.ja: ナムク・ケマル

bio:
Muhammad Namik Kamal 
He is a famous Turkish writer, journalist, and statesman. He is also a poet, one of the pioneers of Turkish nationalism, and belongs to the Young Ottomans movement.
bio_ar_original:
محمد نامق كمال 
وهو أديب تركي مشهور، وصحفي، ورجل دولة، كما أنه شاعر من رواد القومية التركية وينتمي لحركة العثمانيين الشباب.

full: Asma'u bint Usman dan Fodio al-Fulani
primaryTitle: Pioneer of Mass Female Education in West Africa
titles: Nana Asma'u · Mujaddida · Daughter of the Shehu
bio: Asma'u bint Usman dan Fodio al-Fulani (1793–1864) was a scholar, based in Sokoto. Known as Pioneer of Mass Female Education in West Africa.

full: Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Tusi
primaryTitle: Khwaja Nasir — Master of the Wise
titles: Khwaja Nasir · Ustadh al-Bashar (Teacher of Humanity) · Muhaqqiq al-Tusi

names_i18n.ja: ナスィールッディーン・トゥースィー

bio: Al-Khawaja Nasir al-Din Abu Ja`far Muhammad bin Muhammad bin al-Hasan al-Tusi, known as Nasir al-Din al-Tusi or the investigator al-Tusi, a scholar An astronomer, biologist, chemist, mathematician, philosopher, doctor, physicist, theologian, and Persian Shiite authority.
bio_ar_original: اَلْخَوَاجَةُ نَصِيرُ اَلدِّينِ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ اَلْحَسَنِ اَلطُّوسِيُّ اَلْمَعْرُوفُ بنَصِيرِ اَلدِّينِ اَلطُّوسِيِّ أو اَلْمُحَقِّقِ اَلطُّوسِيِّ، عالِمٌ فَلَكِيٌّ، وأَحْيَائِيٌّ، وكِيمِيائِيٌّ، ورِياضِيَّاتِيٌّ، وفَيْلَسُوفٌ، وطبَِيبٌ، وفِيزِيائيٌّ، ومُتَكَلِّمٌ، ومَرْجِعٌ شِيعِيٌّ فَارِسِيٌّ.

full: Abu Mu'in Nasir ibn Khusraw ibn Harith al-Qubadiyani
titles: Hujjat-i Khorasan (Proof of Khorasan) · Greatest Ismaili philosopher-poet

names_i18n.ja: ナースィル・ホスロー

bio: Nasir Khusraw al-Qabadiani al-Maruzi is a Persian traveler, poet, and philosopher. He embraced the Ismaili Shiite doctrine and worked as a preacher for it. He is the author of the Book of Travels, or Safarnama, in which he recorded news of his travels throughout the Islamic world. It was distinguished by an accurate description of Jerusalem and a rare description of the conditions of central and eastern Arabia during the days of the Qarmatians and the Ukhaydarians in the fifth century AH. The book is in Persian and is quite popular in Iran, but it has also been translated into Arabic.
bio_ar_original: ناصر خسرو القبادیاني المروزي رحالة وشاعر وفيلسوف فارسي، اعتنق المذهب الشيعي الإسماعيلي وعمل داعيًا له. له كتاب الأسفار أو السفرنامة، الذي دوّن فيه أخبار أسفاره في أرجاء العالم الإسلامي، وامتاز بوصف دقيق لبيت المقدس ووصف نادر لأحوال وسط الجزيرة العربية وشرقها أيام القرامطة والأخيضريين في القرن الخامس الهجري. الكتاب باللغة الفارسية ويتمتع بقدر من الشعبية في إيران، إلا أنه مترجم أيضًا إلى العربية.

full: Sayyid Nasir al-Din Mahmud 'Chiragh-i Delhi' al-Chishti
titles: Chiragh-e-Delhi (Lamp of Delhi) · Last great Chishti master of Delhi · Refused to leave Delhi during invasions
bio: Sayyid Nasir al-Din Mahmud 'Chiragh-i Delhi' al-Chishti (1274–1356) was a mystic of the Chishti tradition, based in Ayodhya. Known as Chiragh-e-Delhi — Lamp of Delhi.

primaryTitle: Pioneer of Hermeneutic Approach to the Quran

names_i18n.ja: ナスル・アブー・ザイド

bio: Nasr Hamed Abu Zeid is an Egyptian academic and researcher specializing in Islamic studies and specializing in Arabic jurisprudence and humanities.
bio_ar_original: نصر حامد أبو زيد أكاديمي مصري، وباحث متخصص في الدراسات الإسلامية ومتخصص في فقه اللغة العربية والعلوم الإنسانية.

bio: Nasr bin al-Harith (b. c. 594 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of Badr.

names_i18n.ar: نوفل بن عبد الله بن ندلة
names_i18n.ur: نوفل بن عبد الله بن ندلہ

bio: Naufal bin 'Abdullah bin Nadla (c. 594–625 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of Badr.

Nawfal bin Al-Harith bin Abdul Muttalib was a companion and a cousin of the Prophet Muhammad. He was the oldest of Banu Hashim in his time, and his conversion to Islam was delayed until the fifth year of the Hijri year, after which he witnessed the conquest of Mecca and the conquests of Hunayn and Taif.
bio_ar_original: نوفل بن الحارث بن عبد المطلب صحابي، وابن عم للنبي محمد. كان أسن بني هاشم في زمانه، وتأخر إسلامه حتى العام الخامس الهجري، ثم شهد بعدها فتح مكة وغزوتي حنين والطائف.

primaryTitle: Voice of the Tulip Era — Greatest Ottoman Lyric Poet

names_i18n.ja: ネディーム

bio: Aḥmed Nedīm Efendi (c. 1681–1730) was a poet of the Persian Poetry tradition, based in Istanbul. Known as Voice of the Tulip Era — Greatest Ottoman Lyric Poet.

primaryTitle: Greatest Ottoman Panegyrist — Master of the Qasida
bio: Nafi is a Turkish poet. He was born in the town of Hasankale, today called “Basinler”, in the city of Erzurum.
bio_ar_original: نفعي هو شاعر تركي، وُلد في بلدة حسن قلعة المُسماة اليوم «باسينلر» ، من أعمال أرضروم.

titles: Shaykh al-Tariqa · Niyazi
bio: Mehmed ibn Ali Niyazi-i Misri (1618–1694) was a poet of the Qalandari tradition, based in Malatya. Known as Shaykh of Bursa.

full: Hasan ibn Ali ibn Ishaq al-Tusi Nizam al-Mulk
titles: Khwaja Buzurg · Nizam al-Mulk

names_i18n.ja: ニザームルムルク

bio: Qawam al-Din Abu Ali al-Hasan bin Ali bin Ishaq bin al-Abbas al-Tusi, nicknamed Khwaja Bazak, meaning Nizam al-Mulk, born in Tus, in Khorasan, one of the most famous Seljuk ministers. He was a minister to Sultan Alp Arslan and then a minister to his son Jalal al-Dawla Malikshah. He was not a brilliant minister. And just a skilled politician; Rather, he was an advocate of knowledge and literature and a lover of them. He established schools known under his name as “Nizamiya Schools” and circulated them in order to bring together scholars and the state administration. He paid salaries to them, and attracted to them the most prominent jurists and hadith scholars, led by Hujjat al-Islam Abu Hamid al-Ghazali. The school developed in several cities as an institution and worked to spread Sharia, Sunni sciences, mathematics, and astronomy, and he always attended meetings of religious scholars. It is known that if he heard the call to prayer, he would stop what he was doing and stand up to pray, and if the muezzin was negligent in the call to prayer, he would warn him.
bio_ar_original: قَوَّامُ الدِّينِ أَبُو عَلِيّ الحَسَن بْنُ عَلِيّ بْنِ إِسْحَاقِ بْنِ العَبَّاسِ الطُوسِي الملقب بـ خواجة بزك أي نظام المُلك، من مواليد طوس، في خُراسان أحد أشهر وزراء السلاجقة، كان وزيرًا للسلطان ألب أرسلان ثم وزيرا لابنه جلال الدولة ملكشاه، ولم يكن وزيرًا لامعًا وسياسيًّا ماهرًا فحسب؛ بل كان داعيًا للعلم والأدب محبًّا لهما؛ أنشأ المدارس المعروفة باسمه «المدارس النظَّاميَّة» وعممها من أجل الجمع بين العلماء وإدارة الدولة، وأجرى لها الرواتب، وجذب إليها كبار الفقهاء والمحدِّثين، وفي مقدِّمتهم حُجَّة الإسلام أبو حامد الغزالي. تطورت المدرسة في عدة مدن كمؤسسة وعملت على نشر الشريعة والعلوم السنية والرياضيات وعلم الفلك، وكان يحضر اجتماعات علماء الدين دائمًا. ومعلوم أنه كان إذا سمع صوت الأذان يتوقف عما يفعل ويقوم للصلاة، وإذا تهاون المؤذن في الأذان كان يحذره.

full: Jamal al-Din Abu Muhammad Ilyas ibn Yusuf Nizami Ganjawi
primaryTitle: Hakim Nizami — Master of the Persian Romantic Epic
titles: Hakim Nizami · Master of the Khamsa (Five Treasuries)

names_i18n.ja: ニザーミー

bio: Nizami Ganjavi was a 12th century Muslim poet. Nizami is considered the greatest romantic epic poet in Persian literature, credited with bringing the realistic vernacular style to the Persian epic. His heritage is widely appreciated and shared in Afghanistan, the Republic of Azerbaijan, Iran, the Kurdistan Region and Tajikistan.
bio_ar_original: نظامي الكنجوي شاعر مسلم من القرن الثاني عشر. يعتبر نظامي أعظم شاعر ملحمي رومانسي في الأدب الفارسي، يعود له الفضل في جلب الأسلوب العامي الواقعي للملحمة الفارسية. يحظى تراثه بتقدير ومشاركة على نطاق واسع في كل من أفغانستان، جمهورية أذربيجان، إيران، إقليم كردستان وطاجيكستان.

titles: Mahbub-e-Ilahi (Beloved of God) · Sultan al-Mashaikh (Sultan of Spiritual Masters)

names_i18n.ja: ニザームッディーン・アウリヤー

bio: Nizamuddin Auliya is a writer and Sufi of the Chishtiya sect, from India, and he is one of the most famous Sufis from the Indian subcontinent.
bio_ar_original: نظام الدين أولياء هو أديب ومتصوف للطائفة الجشتية، من أهل الهند، وهو أحد أشهر الصوفيين من شبه القارة الهندية.

names_i18n.zh: 尼扎尔·伊本·马阿德·伊本·阿德南

bio:
Nizar bin Ma'ad, the eighteenth grandfather of the Prophet Muhammad bin Abdullah.
Ibn Jarir al-Tabari said: It was said that Nizar was nicknamed Abu Iyad, and it was said that he was nicknamed Abu Rabi’ah. Al-Baladhuri said: Nizar bin Ma`d is nicknamed Abu Haidah.
bio_ar_original:
نزار بن معد، الجد الثامن عشر للنبي محمد بن عبد الله.
قال ابن جرير الطبري: قيل إن نزاراً كان يكنى أبا إياد، وقيل بل كان يكنى أبا ربيعة. قال البلاذري: نزار بن معد يكنى أبا حيدة.

bio: Nu'man bin Malik (c. 594–625 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of Badr.

primaryTitle: Shaykh al-Anbiya — Elder of the Prophets
titles: Nuh · Shaykh al-Anbiya · Abd al-Shakur (the Grateful Servant)
era_label: Early Bronze Age / Mesopotamian Flood Horizon
dating_source: Woolley's Ur flood layer (c.2900 BCE); Finkelstein & Silberman
dating_note: Nuh/Noah traditionally linked to Mesopotamian flood narratives; Woolley dated a major Ur flood deposit to ~2900 BCE.
bio: Nūḥ ibn Lamak ibn Mattushalakh (legendary–legendary) was a prophet, based in Mesopotamia. Known as Shaykh al-Anbiya — Elder of the Prophets.

full: Nur al-Din Mahmud ibn Imad al-Din Zangi
titles: Al-Malik al-Adil · Al-Shahid
bio: Al-Malik al-Adil Abu al-Qasim Nūr al-Dīn Maḥmūd bin Imad al-Dīn Zengī, commonly known as Nur ad-Din or Nureddin, and al-Malik al-Adil, was a Turkoman member of the Zengid dynasty, who ruled the Syrian province of the Seljuk Empire. He reigned from 1146 to 1174. He is regarded as an important figure of the Second Crusade.

full: Nūr Jahān bint Mīrzā Ghiyāth Beg, born Mehr-un-Nissa
primaryTitle: Light of the World — Most Powerful Mughal Empress
classif: Mughal Empress / Poet / Patron of Architecture
titles: Nūr Jahān (Light of the World)

names_i18n.ja: ヌール・ジャハーン

bio: Nur Jahan, born Mehr-un-Nissa, was the twentieth wife and chief consort of the Mughal emperor Jahangir.

titles: Shaykh al-Islam · Imam al-Alam

names_i18n.ja: ヌルディン・アル・ラニリ

bio: Nur al-Din bin Ali al-Raniri was a Sufi Islamic scholar from Randhir in the Surat District of Gujarat, in India, who worked for several years in the court of the Sultan of Aceh in what is now Indonesia. He was the most prolific author at the Acehnese court, and helped contribute to its international reputation as a center of scholarship. His work is considered the oldest Islamic scholarship in Southeast Asia.
bio_ar_original: نور الدين بن علي الرانيري كان عالما إسلاميا صوفيا ومن راندير في محافظة سورت من ولاية غوجارات، في الهند، الذي عمل لعدة سنوات في بلاط سلطان اتشيه في ما هو الآن إندونيسيا. كان أكثر المؤلفين غزارةً في بلاط آتشيه، وساعد في المساهمة في سمعتها الدولية كمركز للمنح الدراسية. واعتبر عمله أقدم منحة دراسية إسلامية في جنوب شرق آسيا.

bio: Nusayba bint Abi Talha (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.
Nusayba bint Niyar (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.

full: Ghiyāth al-Dīn Abū al-Fatḥ ʿUmar ibn Ibrāhīm Khayyām Nīshābūrī

names_i18n.ja: ウマル・ハイヤーム

bio: Ghayat al-Din Abu al-Futuh Omar bin Ibrahim bin Salih al-Khayyam al-Naysaburi (1048–1131 AD), or Omar Khayyam for short, and Khayyam was the nickname of his father, who used to make tents. He was a Persian Muslim astronomer, mathematician, philosopher, and poet, and some say that His origins are Arab. He was born in the city of Nishapur in Khorasan between 1038 and 1048 AD, and died there between 1123 and 1124 when he was 83 years old. He specialized in mathematics, astronomy, language, jurisprudence, and history. He was the first to invent the method of calculating triangles and solving third-degree algebraic equations by cutting a conic, and he is the author of the famous Quadrilaterals.
bio_ar_original: غِيَاثُ الدِّينِ أبو الفُتُوحِ عُمَرُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ صَالِحٍ الخَيَّامُ النَّيْسَابُورِيُّ (1048–1131 مـ) أو عُمَرُ الخَيَّامُ اختصارا، والخيّام لقب والده الذي كان يصنع الخيام، هو عالم فلك ورياضيات وفيلسوف وشاعر فارسي مسلم، ويذهب بعضهم إلى أن أصوله عربية. وُلِدَ في مدينة نيسابور من خراسان ما بين 1038 و1048 مـ، وتوفي فيها ما بين 1123 و1124 وهو بعمر 83 عاما. تخصَّص في الرياضيات والفلك واللغة والفقه والتاريخ، وهو أول من اخترع طريقة حساب المثلثات وحل المعادلات الجبرية التي من الدرجة الثالثة بواسطة قطع المخروط وهو صاحب الرباعيات المشهورة.

titles: Bey · Gazi · Founder of Ottoman Dynasty

names_i18n.ja: オスマン1世

bio: The Father of Kings, Sultan Al-Ghazi Fakhr al-Din Qara Othman Khan bin Ertugrul bin Sulaimanshah al-Qaywi al-Turkmani, known for short as “Othman I” and also known as Othman Bey and Qara Othman; He is the leader of the Turkish Kayi clan, the Rum Seljuk ruler of one of the Anatolian border emirates, and the founder of the Ottoman dynasty that ruled the Balkans, Anatolia, the Arab Levant, and the Lower and Middle Maghrebs for six hundred years until its term expired with the declaration of the establishment of the Turkish Republic in 1922 AD.
bio_ar_original: أَبُو المُلُوك السُّلطان الغازي فخرُ الدين قره‌عُثمان خان بن أرطُغرُل بن سُليمانشاه القايوي التُركماني ، المعروف اختصارًا بـ«عُثمان الأوَّل» ويُعرف كذلك باسم عُثمان بك وقره‌عُثمان ؛ هو زعيم عشيرة قايى التُركيَّة وعامل سلاجقة الروم على إحدى إمارات الثُغور الأناضوليَّة ومُؤسس السُلالة العُثمانيَّة التي حكمت البلقان والأناضول والمشرق العربي والمغربين الأدنى والأوسط طيلة ستمائة عام إلى أن انقضى أجلها مع إعلان قيام الجُمهوريَّة التُركيَّة سنة 1922م.

primaryTitle: Greatest Female Persian Poet of the 20th Century

names_i18n.ja: パルヴィーン・エーテサーミー

bio: Parvin Etesami, a contemporary Iranian poet born in the city of Tabriz, is considered one of the symbols of modern Persian poetry, as Persian linguist Ali Akbar Dehkhoda said.
bio_ar_original: بروين اعتصامي  شاعرة إيرانية معاصرة ولدت في مدينة تبريز تعتبر من رموز الشعر الفارسي الحديث كما قال اللغوي الفارسي علي أكبر دهخدا.

titles: Pir Sultan · Abdal
bio: Haydar / Pir Sultan Abdal (c. 1480–c. 1550) was a poet of the Qalandari tradition, based in Sivas. Known as Martyred Poet of the Alevis.

full: Ḥājī Aḥmad Muḥyiddīn Pīrī ibn Ḥājī Meḥmed, known as Pīrī Reʾīs

names_i18n.ja: ピーリー・レイース

bio: Ahmed Muhyiddin Piri, known in Turkish as “Piri Reis” (born between 1465 and 1470 and died between 1554 and 1555). He is an Ottoman Muslim sea captain and cartographer of Turkish nationality, and he is credited with being the true discoverer of America after his maps were revealed that acknowledge this.
bio_ar_original: أحمد محيي الدين بيري الشهير بالتركية بـ «بيري ريس» ‏(ولد ما بين 1465 و1470 وتوفي ما بين 1554 و1555). هو قبطانٌ بحريٌ وراسم خرائط عثمانيٍ مسلمٍ من القومية التركية ، ويُنسب له أنه مكتشف أمريكا الحقيقي بعد الكشف عن خرائط له تقر بذلك.

primaryTitle: Seal of the Prophets · Mercy to the Worlds · al-Mustafa
titles: Rasul Allah · al-Mustafa · al-Amin · al-Sadiq · Shafi al-Mudhnibeen · Rahmatul lil Alameen · Habib Allah · Khayru l-Khalq · Sayyid al-Mursalin · al-Nabi al-Ummi

quranRef.epithet: Seal of the Prophets · The Praised One · Mercy to the Worlds

dateNote: Born in the Year of the Elephant (Aam al-Fil). Dates follow the Islamic historical tradition.
dating_source: Ibn Ishaq Sirat Rasul Allah (via Ibn Hisham); al-Tabari 'History'; multiple contemporary and near-contemporary attestations
dating_note: Fully historical. Born in Mecca 570 CE (Year of the Elephant); died in Medina 8 June 632 CE. Dates firmly established in Islamic and non-Islamic sources.

names_i18n.ja: ムハンマド・イブン＝アブドゥッラーフ

bio: Abu Al-Qasim Muhammad bin Abdullah bin Abdul Muttalib Al-Qurashi Al-Hashimi; He is the Messenger of God to mankind and jinn in Islam. He was sent to return the worlds to the monotheism and worship of God, like all prophets and messengers, and He is their seal. He was sent to all people, and Muslims believe that He is the most honorable of creatures and the master of mankind, and they believe in infallibility. When mentioning his name, Muslims append the phrase “may God bless him and grant him peace” with the addition of “and his family” and “and his companions” at times, as stated in the Qur’an and the Sunnah of the Prophet, which urges them to pray for him. The Prophet Muhammad left a great impact on the souls of Muslims, and the manifestations of their love and veneration for him increased in their following of his command, his way of life, and his worship of God, and their memorizing his words, deeds, and attributes, and collecting this in books known as books of the biography and hadith of the Prophet, and some of them celebrated his birth in the month of Rabi’ al-Awwal every year. Muhammad united Arabia into a single Islamic system, with the Qur’an and its teachings and practices forming the basis of its system.
bio_ar_original: أَبُو الْقَاسِمِ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْمُطَّلِبِ الْقُرَشِيُّ الْهَاشِمِيُّ ؛ هو رسول الله إلى الإنس والجن في الإسلام؛ أُرسِل ليُعيد العالمين إلى توحيد الله وعبادته شأنه شأن كل الأنبياء والمرسلين، وهو خاتمهم، وأُرسِل للناس كافة، ويُؤمن المسلمون بأنه أشرف المخلوقات وسيد البشر، ويعتقدون فيه العصمة. عند ذكر اسمه، يُلحِق المسلمون عبارة «صلى الله عليه وسلم» مع إضافة «وآله» و«وصحبه» في بعض الأحيان، لِمَا جاء في القرآن والسنة النبوية مما يحثهم على الصلاة عليه. ترك النبي محمد أثرًا كبيرًا في نفوس المسلمين، وكثرت مظاهر محبّتهم وتعظيمهم له باتباعهم لأمره وأسلوب حياته وتعبده لله، وقيامهم بحفظ أقواله وأفعاله وصفاته وجمع ذلك في كتب عُرفت بكتب السيرة والحديث النبوي، واحتفال بعضهم بمولده في شهر ربيعٍ الأولِ في كل عام. وحَّد محمد الجزيرة العربية في نظام إسلامي واحد، حيث شكّل القرآن وتعاليمه وممارساته أساس نظامها.

bio: Qasim Amin was a modern Islamist, and one of the founders of the Egyptian National Movement and Cairo University. Qasim Amin is historically viewed as one of the "first feminists" in the Arab world, although he joined the conversation about women's rights very late in its development, and "feminism" has been the subject of scholarly debate.
bio_ar_original: قاسم أمين كان إسلاميًا حديثًا، وأحد مؤسسي الحركة الوطنية المصرية وجامعة القاهرة، يُنظر إلى قاسم أمين تاريخيًا على أنه واحد من «النسويين الأوائل» في العالم العربي، على الرغم من أنه انضم إلى الحديث حول حقوق المرأة في وقت متأخر جدًا من تطوره، وكانت «النسوية» موضوع جدل علمي.

bio: Blind Basran scholar renowned for his prodigious memory. A leading Tabi'i in Quranic exegesis, hadith, and Arabic language. Studied under Anas ibn Malik and al-Hasan al-Basri. Authored early works on Quranic abrogation and pilgrimage rites. Died during a plague in Wasit.

full: Qusayy ibn Kilāb ibn Murra al-Qurashī
primaryTitle: Reunifier of the Quraysh — Founder of Meccan Governance
titles: Qusayy · Mujammi' (the Gatherer) · great-great-grandfather of Muhammad
bio: Qusai ibn Kilab ibn Murrah, also spelled Qusayy, Kusayy, Kusai, or Cossai, born Zayd, was an Ishmaelite descendant of Abraham. Orphaned early on, he would rise to become chief of Mecca, and leader of the Quraysh tribe. He is best known for being an ancestor of the Islamic prophet Muhammad as well as the third and the fourth Rashidun caliphs, Uthman and Ali, and the later Umayyad, Abbasid, and Fatimid caliphs along with several of the most prominent Hashemite dynasties in the orient.

primaryTitle: Christian Polymath of the Islamic Golden Age

names_i18n.ja: クスター・イブン・ルーカー

bio: Abu Al-Saqr Ismail bin Bulbul Qusta bin Luqa Al-Baalbaki Al-Baghdadi, and it was said that Abu Ubaidullah bin Yahya Al-Baalbaki Al-Baghdadi, known as Qusta bin Luqa or Qastos, is a scientist in medicine, astronomy, mathematics, nature, and botany in the golden age of Islam. He is an Orthodox Christian, from the city of Baalbek during its Abbasid era. He is called a Greek due to his Greek (Roman) origins. Along with his Arabic language, he learned Greek and Syriac. He translated many Greek books and wrote many works.
bio_ar_original: أبو الصقر إسماعيل بن بلبل قسطا بن لوقا البعلبكي البغدادي وقيل أبو عبيد الله بن يحيى البعلبكي البغدادي المعروف بـقسطا بن لوقا أو قسطوس هو عالم بالطب والفلك والرياضيات والطبيعة والنبات في العصر الذهبي للإسلام، وهو مسيحي أرثذوكسي، من مدينة بعلبك في عهدها العباسي. ويلقب باليوناني نسبةً إلى أصوله اليونانية (الرومية). وتعلم مع لغته العربية اللغتين اليونانية والسريانية، فترجم كثيرا من الكتب اليونانية، وله تصانيف كثيرة.

primaryTitle: Female Emigrant and Narrator of Hadith

titles: Allama al-A'zam

names_i18n.ja: クトゥブッディーン・シーラーズィー

bio: Qutb al-Din Mahmud ibn Diya al-Din Masoud ibn Muslih al-Shirazi (1236–1311) was a Persian scholar and poet who lived in the thirteenth century. He played a major role in astronomy, mathematics, medicine, physics, music theory, philosophy, and Sufism.
bio_ar_original: قطب الدين محمود بن ضياء الدين مسعود بن مصلح الشيرازي (1236–1311) هو عالم وشاعر فارسي عاش في القرن الثالث عشر. كان له دورًا كبيرًا في علم الفلك والرياضيات والطب والفيزياء ونظرية الموسيقى والفلسفة والصوفية.

primaryTitle: From the First Pledge of Aqabah
bio: Qutba bin 'Amir bin Hadida (c. 594–650 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as From the First Pledge of Aqabah.

titles: Qutb al-Din (Pole of the Faith) · Qutb al-Delhi

names_i18n.ja: クトゥブッディーン・バフティヤール・カーキー

bio: Qutb al-Aqtab and Qutb al-Din Khwaja Sayyid Muhammad Bakhtiyar al-Hussaini al-Khaki was a Sunni Muslim Sufi, saint and scholar of the Chishtiya order from Delhi, in India. He was a disciple and Sufi successor of Mu'in ad-Din Chishti as head of the Chishti order. Before him, the Chishti regime in India was limited to Ajmer and Nagaur. He played a major role in establishing the regime securely in Delhi. His shrine is located next to Zafar Mahal in Mehrauli, which is also the place of his annual wedding celebrations. The wedding was greatly appreciated by many rulers of Delhi such as Iltutmish who built a nearby well, Gandhak Ki Baoli for him, Sher Shah Suri who built a grand gate, Bahadur Shah I who built the nearby Moti Mosque, and Farrukhsiyar who added a marble screen and mosque.
bio_ar_original: قطب الأقطاب وقطب الدين خواجة السيد محمد بختيار الحسيني الكاكي كان صوفيًا مسلمًا سنيًا، ووليًّا وباحثًا في الطريقة الجشتية من دلهي، في الهند. كان تلميذًا وخليفة صوفيًّا لمعين الدين الجشتي كرئيس للطريقة الجشتية. قبله كان النظام الجشتي في الهند يقتصر على أجمر وناجور. لعب دورًا رئيسًا في ترسيخ النظام بشكل آمن في دلهي. يقع ضريحه بجوار ظفر محل في مهراولي، وهو أيضًا مكان احتفالاته السنوية بالعرس. كان العرس موضع تقدير كبير من العديد من حكام دلهي مثل إلتميش الذي بنى بئرًا قريبًا، وغاندهاك كي باولي له، وشير شاه صوري الذي بنى بوابة كبيرة، وبهادور شاه الأول الذي بنى مسجد مَوتي القريب، وفاروخسيار الذي أضاف شاشة رخامية ومسجدًا.

bio: Rā'ū ibn Fāligh (legendary–legendary) was a founding figure, based in Mesopotamia. Known as Ancestor of Prophet Muhammad. The sources record 2 teachers.
Rabi' bin Iyas b 'Amr (b. c. 594 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of Badr.
Rabi'a bin Aktham al-Asadi (b. c. 594 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Companion of Badr.

Rabia bin Al-Harith bin Abdul Muttalib is a companion and a cousin of the Prophet Muhammad. His conversion to Islam was delayed until the fifth year of the Prophet’s Hijra, and he witnessed with the Prophet Muhammad the conquest of Mecca, and the battles of Hunayn and Taif, and he died during the caliphate of Omar ibn al-Khattab in Medina.
bio_ar_original: ربيعة بن الحارث بن عبد المطلب صحابي، وابن عم للنبي محمد. تأخر إسلامه إلى السنة الخامسة للهجرة النبوية، وشهد مع النبي محمد فتح مكة، وغزوتي حنين والطائف، وتوفي في خلافة عمر بن الخطاب في المدينة المنورة.

titles: Umm al-Khayr · Sayyidat al-Nisa · Crown of Men

names_i18n.ja: ラービア・アダウィーヤ

bio: Rabi’a al-Adawiya or Rabi’a al-Qaysiyyah, Umm Amr, a woman of the Tabi’in followers, who was famous for her worship, asceticism, and piety.

primaryTitle: al-Manar Editor — Bridge between Salafism and Islamic Modernism

names_i18n.ja: ラシード・リダー

bio: Muhammad Rashid bin Ali Reda, an Islamic thinker, writer and literary figure during the Ottoman era. He was born in the village of Qalamoun in Lebanon, a village located on the Mediterranean coast of Mount Lebanon and about three miles from Tripoli, the Levant. He died in Egypt on Jumada al-Awwal 23, 1354.
bio_ar_original: محمد رشيد بن علي رضا مفكرًا إسلاميًا و كاتبا وأديبا خلال الحقبة العثمانية ، ولد في قرية القلمون بلبنان ، وهي قرية تقع على شاطئ البحر المتوسط من جبل لبنان وتبعد عن طرابلس الشام بنحو ثلاثة أميال، وتوفي بمصر في 23 جمادى الأولى 1354.

titles: Abd al-Wahid Yahya · Sheikh

names_i18n.ja: ルネ・ゲノン

bio: René Jean-Marie-Joseph Guénon, also known as Abdalwahid Yahia, was a French-Egyptian intellectual who wrote on topics ranging from esotericism, "sacred science" and "traditional studies" to symbolism and initiation.

titles: Naqqāsh-bāshī (Chief Painter)

names_i18n.ja: リダー・アッバースィー

bio: Reza Abbasi was a Persian calligrapher of the Isfahan school during the Safavid period, who spent most of his career working for Shah Abbas I. He is considered the last of the craftsmen in this field, and one of his most famous works is marqa or albums, especially drawing young beauties.
bio_ar_original: رضا عباسي كان خطاطا فارسيا من مدرسة أصفهان خلال الفترة الصفوية، قضى معظم حياته المهنية في العمل للشاه عباس الأول. يعتبر أخر الحرفيين في هذا المجال، من أشهر أعماله المرقة أو ألبومات، وخصوصا رسم جمال الشباب.

primaryTitle: Martyr of the Battle of Uhud

Rifa'a bin 'Amr bin Zayd (c. 594–625 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Companion of Badr.

Rifa'a bint Thabit (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.

full: Abū ʿAbd Allāh Jaʿfar ibn Muḥammad Rūdakī
titles: Ustād-i Shaʿirān
bio: Al-Rudaki Abu Abdullah Jaafar bin Muhammad bin Hakim bin Abdul Rahman bin Adam. A Persian poet, considered the first significant poet in the entire history of Persian literature. He was born in the village of Rudak, one of the villages of Samarkand. One of his most famous works is his poetic translation of the book “Kalila and Dimna,” based on the Arabic translation written by Abdullah bin Al-Muqaffa.
bio_ar_original: الرُّودكي أبو عبد الله جعفر بن محمد بن حكيم بن عبد الرحمن بن آدم. شاعر فارسي ، يعتبر أول شاعر ذي شأن في تاريخ الأدب الفارسي كله. ولد في قرية رودك من قرى سمرقند. من أشهر أعماله ترجمته الشعرية لكتاب « كليلة ودمنة » نقلاً عن الترجمة العربية التي وضعها عبد الله بن المقفّع.

bio: Rughayba bint Sahl bin Tha'laba (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.

full: Ruqayya bint Muhammad ibn Abd Allah al-Hashimiyya
primaryTitle: Daughter of the Prophet; First to be Buried in Baqi al-Gharqad

names_i18n.ja: ルカイヤ・ビント・ムハンマド

bio: Ruqaya bint Muhammad, the daughter of the Prophet Muhammad bin Abdullah from his wife Khadija bint Khuwaylid, and his eldest daughter after Zainab. The Prophet married her to Othman bin Affan after Utbah bin Abi Lahab divorced her when his father, Abu Lahab, forced him to divorce her. Ruqayyah was nicknamed Umm Abdullah and her nickname was the one with two migrations. She was born about seven years before the Prophet’s mission. She converted to Islam and immigrated to Abyssinia and Medina. She died on the day of the Battle of Badr in the year 2 AH with her husband, Othman bin Affan, and she was 21 years old at that time.
bio_ar_original: رُقيّة بنت محمد، بنت النبي محمد بن عبد الله من زوجته خديجة بنت خويلد، وأكبر بناته بعد زينب. زوجها النبي لعثمان بن عفان بعد أن طلقها عتبة بن أبي لهب عندما أجبره والده أبو لهب على تطليقها، تُكنى رقية بأم عبد الله ولقبها ذات الهجرتين، وُلدت قبل البعثة النبوية بنحو سبعة أعوام، وأدركت الإسلام وهاجرت إلى الحبشة والمدينة المنورة، وتُوفيت يوم غزوة بدر سنة 2هـ عند زوجها عثمان بن عفان وعمرها يومئذٍ 21 عامًا.

bio: Ruqayya bint Thabit bin Khalid (b. c. 595 CE) was a female companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Ansari Companion of Madinah.

full: Ruzbihan ibn Abi Nasr al-Baqli al-Shirazi
primaryTitle: Shaykh-i Shirin — Master of Intoxicated Love
titles: Shaykh-i Shirin (The Sweet Master) · Commentator on ecstatic utterances (shathiyyat)

names_i18n.ja: ルーズビハーン・バクリー・シーラーズィー

bio: Rozbahan al-Bakli al-Shirazi, who is Rozbahan bin Abi al-Nasr al-Fasawi al-Shirazi al-Kazrouni, Sadr al-Din, Abu Muhammad al-Bakli. A Sufi was born in Bassa, near Shiraz. He died in 606 AH, or it was said 666 AH.
bio_ar_original: روزبهان البقلي الشيرازي وهو روزبهان بن أبي النصر الفسوي الشيرازي الكازروني، صدر الدين، أبو محمد البقلي. صوفي ولد في «بسا» قرب شيرازتُوفي 606 هـ وقيل 666 هـ.

bio: Sa'd bin Khalifa (c. 597–636 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Martyr of the Battle of Uhud.

full: Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas ibn Wuhayb al-Zuhri al-Qurashi
primaryTitle: Faris al-Islam — Knight of Islam; Conqueror of Persia
titles: Faris al-Islam · One of the Ten Promised Paradise · First Archer of Islam

names_i18n.ja: サアド・イブン・アビー・ワッカース

bio: Abu Ishaq Saad bin Abi Waqqas Malik Al-Zuhri Al-Qurashi, one of the ten promised Paradise, and one of the first to convert to Islam. It was said that he was the third to convert to Islam, and it was said that he was the seventh, and he was the first to shoot an arrow for the sake of God, and the Prophet said to him: “Throw, may my father and mother be sacrificed for you.” He is one of the uncles of the Prophet, and one of the six companions of the Shura Council whom Omar ibn Al-Khattab chose to choose the caliph after him.
bio_ar_original: أبو إسحاق سَعْد بن أَبي وقاص مَالِك الزهري القرشي ، أحد العشرة المبشرين بالجنة، ومن السابقين الأولين إلى الإسلام، وقيل أنه ثالث من أسلم وقيل السابع، وهو أوّل من رمى بسهمٍ في سبيل الله، وقال له النبي: «ارم فداك أبي وأمي»، وهو من أخوال النبي، وأحد الستة أصحاب الشورى الذين اختارهم عمر بن الخطاب ليختاروا الخليفة من بعده.

primaryTitle: From the First Pledge of Aqabah
bio: Sa'd ibn ar-Rabi' (c. 594–625 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as From the First Pledge of Aqabah.

full: Sa'd ibn Muadh ibn al-Numan ibn Imru al-Qays al-Ansari al-Awsi
primaryTitle: Sayyid al-Aws — Chief of the Aws Tribe
dod_s: 627 CE / 5 AH — from wounds at Battle of the Trench
classif: Companion / Tribal Chief / Ansar Leader / Martyr
titles: Sayyid al-Aws (Chief of the Aws tribe) · Siddiq al-Ansar (Truthful Man of the Ansar) · Abu Amr
bio: Sa'd ibn Muadh ibn al-Numan ibn Imru al-Qays al-Ansari al-Awsi (c. 590 CE–627 CE / 5 AH) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Medina. Known as Sayyid al-Aws — Chief of the Aws Tribe.

full: Sa'd ibn Ubadah ibn Dulaym al-Ansari al-Khazraji
primaryTitle: Sayyid al-Ansar — Chief of the Helpers
titles: Sayyid al-Ansar · Chief of Khazraj

names_i18n.ja: サアド・ブン・ウバーダ

bio: Saad bin Ubadah Al-Saadi Al-Khazraj Al-Ansari, Abu Thabit, and it was said: Abu Qais, one of the most honorable Arab houses, the most ancient in lineage, and he was the leader of the Khazraj before Islam. A companion of mine who converted to Islam early, witnessed the Pledge of Aqaba, lived next to the Messenger Muhammad, and was famous for his generosity and generosity along with his son Qais bin Saad bin Ubadah Al-Saadi. It was said about Saad: “A man from the Ansar would go to his home, with one of the immigrants, or with two, or with three. Saad bin Ubadah used to set off with eighty.
bio_ar_original: سَعْدُ بْنُ عُبَادَةَ اَلسَّاعِدِيُّ اَلْخَزْرَجِيُّ اَلْأَنْصَارِيُّ، أَبُو ثَابِتٍ، وقيل: أَبُو قَيْسٍ، من أكرم بيوت العرب، وأعرقِها نسبًا، وهو زعيم الخزرج قبل الإسلام. صحابي أسلم مبكراً، وشهد بيعة العقبة، وعاش إلى جوار الرسول محمد، واشتهر بالكرم والجود هو وابنه قيس بن سعد بن عبادة الساعدي، وقيل عن سعد: «كان الرجل من الأنصار ينطلق إلى داره، بالواحد من المهاجرين، أو بالاثنين، أو بالثلاثة. وكان سعد بن عبادة ينطلق بالثمانين».

famous: Sa'd Mawla Hatib bin Abi Balta'ah
full: Sa'd Mawla Hatib bin Abi Balta'ah

names_i18n.ar: سعد مولى حاطب بن أبي بلتعة
names_i18n.ur: سعد مولیٰ حاطب بن ابی بلتعہ

bio: Sa'd Mawla Hatib bin Abi Balta'ah (b. c. 594 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Companion of Badr.

names_i18n.ar: سعيد بن عبد قيس بن لقيط
names_i18n.ur: سعید بن عبد قیس بن لقیط

bio: Sa'id bin 'Abd Qays bin Laqit (b. c. 593 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Emigrant to Abyssinia.
Sa'id bin 'Amr al-Tamimi (c. 593–634 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Emigrant to Abyssinia.
Sa'id bin al-Harith (c. 593–634 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Mecca. Known as Emigrant to Abyssinia.

primaryTitle: Born Muslim — First Generation Born into Islam
bio: Sa'id bin Khalid ibn Sa'id (b. c. 593 CE) was a companion of the Prophet Muhammad, based in Abyssinia. Known as Born Muslim — First Generation Born into Islam.
